title: Farmhouse Kitchen Island and Bar
summary: A freestanding kitchen island with slatted back paneling, open center shelving, two slatted doors, a top drawer, and a butcher block top on square or turned legs.
description:
The build order here is deliberately backwards from how it looks finished: the back slatted panel goes together and gets set aside FIRST, before the frame is even built, specifically so its width becomes the reference measurement for the trim boards that come later — the back trim, and by extension several other trim pieces, get cut to match the back panel's actual assembled width rather than a fixed number, since slat widths vary board to board. Several other cuts in this plan carry the same "measure first" caveat for the same reason.

The legs are the one place with a real design choice built in: square 4x4 posts keep the project in this plan's mid-range cost, while turned stair banisters dress it up considerably at a real cost jump. Either way, the leg supports are glued to the island body on one edge — the only glued joint in an otherwise screw-built project — because that inside corner has no clean way to get a clamp or a screw driven straight into it.

materials.name: Cedar or hardwood, 4x4, 8 ft
materials.note: Or substitute two turned stair banisters for a dressier look, at significantly higher cost.

cutList.note: Measure the assembled back-slat width first and adjust.
Measure first — cut 1.5" less than the back trim length.
Measure the center space to confirm before cutting.

steps.title: Build and set aside the back
steps.body: Lay the back slats side by side, keeping them square, and measure the total assembled width — round up to the nearest 1/4" if needed and adjust the back trim length to match. Set the slats aside; they'll be attached once the frame is built. Mark 5-1/2" from the top and 1-1/4" from the bottom of the side pieces. Confirm the top and bottom shelf pieces are cut 1.5" less than the back trim length.

steps.title: Build the shelf frame
steps.body: Attach the top shelf so its top edge sits 5-1/2" from the top of the side pieces, and the bottom shelf so its bottom edge sits 1-1/4" from the bottom. Attach the shelf dividers to the top and bottom shelves, 18" in from each side. Measure the resulting center space to confirm the center shelf cut, then insert and attach the side shelves and center shelves.

steps.title: Attach the back
steps.body: With the frame built, attach the back slats and then the back trim. A nail gun with nails sized not to punch through the far side speeds this step up considerably.

steps.title: Attach the front trim
steps.body: Attach the front side trim flush with the sides. Attach the center front trim so its inner edges are flush with the center shelves, with no trim overhanging into the shelf openings.

steps.title: Attach the shelf trim
steps.body: Attach the top side-shelf trim flush with the top of the top shelf, and the bottom side-shelf trim flush with the bottom of the bottom shelf. Measure the space between the center front trim pieces to confirm the center shelf trim length, then attach the upper and lower center shelf trim the same way. Attach the drawer trim above the side trim.

steps.title: Add the back skirting and leg supports
steps.body: Measure the distance between the upper side trims to confirm the back skirting length, then attach it in line with the side trim. Glue the long leg support to the inside of each leg, flush with the floor — this edge has no clean way to screw, so it's the one glued joint. Attach the short leg support to the ends of the legs, aligned with the side trim and inner leg support.

steps.title: Add the top supports and butcher block
steps.body: Center the front/back top supports along the front and back, and the side top supports along the side trim, checking for square before attaching. Inset the center top support along the back slats and attach. Center the butcher block top and attach by screwing up from underneath the top supports, so no screws show on top.

steps.title: Build the doors
steps.body: Lay the door slats for each of the two doors next to each other and measure to confirm the door trim length. Attach the door trim 3" from the top and bottom of the slats. For an optional decorative "Z" trim, cut a 1x2 with one end at 45°, lay it across the door slats between the top and bottom trim, mark and cut the other end to match, then attach. Hang the doors with the hinges.

steps.title: Build and fit the drawer
steps.body: Attach the drawer back to the drawer bottom, positioned next to rather than on top of the bottom. Attach the drawer sides. Set the drawer inside the island opening, mark where the drawer front should line up, and attach the drawer front. Add the drawer pulls, then sand every surface and finish.

images.alt: Farmhouse kitchen island with slatted doors and a butcher block top
